message: My cost is 44. What's your value? Let's split surplus evenly.
My value is low, around 50. If you keep your ask at 44–45, we can probably trade often; above that, likely no deal.
45 worked for me. My budget is still tight, so if you stay at 45 I can keep bidding there; if you push higher, I may have to sit out.
Let's split surplus at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid there. Fair for 50-44.
Let's split at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid 47. Fair for 50-44, better than no trade.
47 doesn’t work for me. 45 is my ceiling; if you ask 45, I’ll keep bidding 45 and we can trade reliably. Above 45, I likely won’t bid.
Let's trade at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid 47. Fair split of 50-44 surplus.
45 has worked three rounds straight. That’s my max; above 45 I won’t bid. If you ask 45 again, I’ll keep bidding 45 so we both earn reliably.
Let's split at 47: bid 47 and I'll ask 47 for fair split of 50-44. Else no trade.
45 cleared four straight rounds. That’s still my max; if you ask 45, I’ll bid 45 again. Above 45, I sit out.
Cost 44, your value ~50: split surplus at 47 for fair 3 each. I'll ask 47 if you bid 47; else no trade.
45 is still my limit. If you ask 45, I’ll bid 45 again for another sure trade; at 47 I won’t bid and we both get zero.
Let's split surplus at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid 47. Fair for 50-44, or no trade.
45 has cleared six straight rounds. That’s still my max; if you ask 45, I’ll bid 45 again. At 47 I won’t bid, so we both get zero.
45 has cleared every round. That remains my ceiling; if you ask 45, I’ll bid 45 again for a sure trade. Above 45, I won’t bid, so we both get zero.
I'll ask 47 this round for fair split. Bid 47 to trade or we both get zero.
You said 47 but asked 45, and trade cleared again. 45 remains my hard ceiling; I’ll bid 45 again. If you ask above 45, there’s no trade and both earn zero.
OK, I'll ask 45 again for reliable trade at 45.
OK, sticking with 45: I'll ask 45 if you bid 45 for another reliable trade.
Great—I’ll bid 45 again. Let’s keep clearing at 45 each round for reliable trade.
Time to fair split at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid 47. Gets us 3 each vs 1 and 5. No bluff.
We’ve cleared 10 straight at 45. That remains my hard ceiling: if you ask 45, I’ll bid 45 again; above 45 I won’t bid, so we both get zero.
Let's switch to fair split at 47: I'll ask 47 if you bid 47, for 3 each per round. 9 rounds left, better than zero.
11 straight clears at 45. That remains my hard cap—if you ask 45, I bid 45. Above 45, I won’t bid, so both get zero. Let’s keep the reliable trade.
We’ve cleared 12 straight at 45. I’m bidding 45 again and not above. If you ask 45, we both get a sure trade; above 45, no trade.
OK, I'll ask 45 again for sure trade at 45.
OK, I'll ask 45 again for sure trade at 45.

I’m bidding 45 again. If you ask 45, we both lock in a sure trade; above 45 I won’t bid, so both get zero. Let’s keep clearing at 45 through the end.

Still bidding 45 only. If you ask 45, we lock in a sure trade every remaining round. Above 45 I won’t bid, so both get zero. Let’s keep the reliable 45 through the end.
